raw_output:
PR #155 review re-run (Claude, Fable 5), chore/v011-release-prep vs origin/dev, scratch worktree, real exit codes: backend pytest 567 passed (coverage 95.53%) exit 0, mypy 0, ruff 0; frontend untouched (6-file diff). NO FINDINGS. VERIFIED: uv.lock P1 fix confirmed — git diff shows a single hunk (somnus editable version 0.1.0->0.1.1), no third-party version changed/added/removed (T-13 clean, no audit obligation); `uv lock --check` exit 0; `uv export --locked --format requirements-txt` exit 0 (the make setup-backend path the P1 broke now succeeds). Version bump complete: backend/main.py VERSION, pyproject.toml, uv.lock, test_health all 0.1.1; grep finds no remaining app-identity 0.1.0 (all other 0.1.0 hits are historical — v0.1.0-acceptance frozen record, PLAN/THREAT_MODEL descope notes, ADR 015, resolved review artifacts). frontend/package.json 0.0.0 correctly left (Vite scaffold default, never versioned). Makefile comment accurate to db_location.py main()'s three branches (configured->report+exit, non-TTY-unconfigured->fallback notice+default, else->prompt). Acceptance-record #122 addition correct (closed 2026-07-20, milestone v0.1.1) and reconciles the pre-existing 21-vs-20 enumeration.

--- Review history ---
Initial battery found a validated P1 (Codex): the version bump left uv.lock recording somnus 0.1.0, so `uv export --locked` (make setup-backend) and CI's `uv lock --check` would fail on a stale lock. Fixed by regenerating uv.lock (commit f998f8c — single line, somnus 0.1.0->0.1.1, zero dependency-resolution churn so no audit obligation) plus a P3 Makefile-comment accuracy fix (583d4fe, non-TTY-unconfigured branch prints a fallback notice, not a location report). Per the ship severity-validated re-run rule, the FULL battery was re-run on the fixed head — this artifact records the re-run, which found no P1/P2/P3/security findings across all four legs.
